briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 14, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis Warnings and Concerns
sections.content: According to recent research, a 'global damage' cost of 280,000 won per ton of greenhouse gas has been calculated, a serious figure derived from 10,000 simulations [1]. Internationally, scientists continue to report that glaciers in Austria are collapsing and disintegrating due to climate change [19, 33], and the phenomenon of Earth's day length increasing at an unprecedented rate is also being attributed to anthropogenic climate change [23, 24, 30]. Particularly concerning is the news that the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is downplaying the importance of climate change by transitioning its water infrastructure programs [20, 28], which has drawn criticism for 'exacerbating the climate crisis' [29].

sections.title: 🌍 Strengthening International Cooperation and Climate Change Response Obligations
sections.content: The international community continues to emphasize cooperation and obligations for responding to climate change. Domestically, National Assembly Speaker Woo Won-shik expressed expectations for tangible outcomes from the 'Korea-Ghana Climate Change Cooperation Agreement' [2], and Korea and Georgia have begun climate change cooperation, securing their first foothold in the Caucasus region [4]. UN experts supported a resolution affirming member states' obligations to respond to climate change, emphasizing international responsibility [22], and discussions are active in various fields, including planned lectures and symposiums on the health impacts of climate change and civil justice [26, 27]. Furthermore, events are being held to address insurance as a policy tool for climate change response [21], and diversified approaches are being taken, such as two universities collaborating on a maple tree adaptation education program for climate change [25].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestic local governments are accelerating various efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals. Yeosu City reviewed the preparations for the UNFCCC Climate Week [3], and Cheongdo County declared a 'Carbon-Neutral Saemaul' to commemorate the 56th anniversary of the Saemaul Undong (New Community Movement) and held related environmental conservation events [5, 10]. Incheon City plans to hold the 'Carbon Neutrality Integrated Committee' and the 'Carbon Neutrality Future City Committee' in 2026 to discuss integrated environmental policies [6, 14]. Furthermore, various local governments, including Daegu City and Cheongju City, are actively recruiting participants for the 'Automobile Carbon Neutrality Point System,' which awards points for reducing vehicle mileage, thereby encouraging citizen participation [7, 8, 9, 12]. Gwangyang City is accelerating practical carbon neutrality efforts, such as creating blue carbon forests to respond to the climate crisis [13].

sections.title: 🌱 Expanding Renewable Energy and Eco-Friendly Infrastructure
sections.content: Investments and efforts to transition to renewable energy and expand eco-friendly infrastructure are actively underway both domestically and internationally. SK Group and KKR are collaborating on renewable energy projects, leading to a re-evaluation of SK Eternix's value [15], and Goldman Sachs has also made significant investments in 'energy storage,' a key area for renewable energy transition [16]. Efforts to expand renewable energy supply, such as solar power support projects, are continuing in domestic industrial complexes [17]. Notably, in Siheung City, the 0.8km-long 'Gyeonggi Sunlight Bicycle Path,' built under solar power facilities, has been completed, presenting an exemplary case of carbon-neutral eco-friendly transportation infrastructure [11, 18].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing served as an opportunity to once again recognize the severity of the climate crisis and to review various response efforts being made both domestically and internationally. This is a critical juncture where global cooperation and action for a sustainable future are becoming even more important. This concludes the climate briefing for March 14, 2026.
